sourceMovie.title: Police Academy 2: Their First Assignment
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Police Academy 2: Their First Assignment for the same reason it broke through in 1985: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Jerry Paris keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: 21 Jump Street
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Police Academy 2: Their First Assignment clicked for you, 21 Jump Street (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Police Academy 2: Their First Assignment?
faq.answer: Try 21 Jump Street, 22 Jump Street, Rush Hour 2—each shares DNA with Police Academy 2: Their First Assignment while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Police Academy 2: Their First Assignment?
faq.answer: Police Academy 2: Their First Assignment (1985) is best described as Comedy, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Police Academy 2: Their First Assignment part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Police Academy Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
